name: TerraformTask
description: Execute terraform commands to manage resources on Microsoft Azure, Amazon Web Services(AWS), Google Cloud Platform(GCP) and Oracle Cloud Infrastructure(OCI).
author: Microsoft Corporation

groups.name: providerAzureRm
groups.displayName: Azure provider configuration (AzureRM, AzureAD and AzAPI)

groups.name: backendAWS
groups.displayName: Amazon Web Services(AWS) S3 backend configuration

groups.name: backendGCP
groups.displayName: Google Cloud Platform(GCP) GCS backend configuration

groups.name: backendHCP
groups.displayName: HCP Terraform / Terraform Cloud backend configuration

inputs.name: provider
inputs.label: Provider
inputs.helpMarkDown:
The name of the provider used in the terraform configuration files.
Only one provider must be specified in the terraform configuration files in the below specified configuration directory.

inputs.name: command
inputs.label: Command
inputs.helpMarkDown: The terraform command to execute.

inputs.name: backendType
inputs.label: State backend type
inputs.visibleRule: command = init
inputs.helpMarkDown: Where Terraform remote state is stored. Independent of the deployment provider above. Defaults to azurerm for backwards compatibility. Use 'Generic / Other' for any backend not listed (http, PostgreSQL, Consul, Kubernetes, etc.).

inputs.name: workingDirectory
inputs.label: Configuration directory
inputs.helpMarkDown: Directory containing the Terraform configuration files. The default value is $(System.DefaultWorkingDirectory)

inputs.name: commandOptions
inputs.label: Additional command arguments
inputs.helpMarkDown:
Any additional arguments for the selected command such as '-option=value' or '-flag'. Multiple options can also be provided delimited by spaces.

Examples:
-out=tfplan (for terraform plan)
tfplan -auto-approve (for terraform apply)

inputs.name: replaceAddress
inputs.label: Replace resource address
inputs.visibleRule: command = plan
inputs.helpMarkDown: Force replacement of a specific resource. Passed as -replace=ADDRESS to terraform plan or apply. Requires Terraform 1.0+.

inputs.name: workspaceSubCommand
inputs.label: Workspace sub-command
inputs.visibleRule: command = workspace
inputs.helpMarkDown: The workspace sub-command to run.

inputs.name: workspaceName
inputs.label: Workspace name
inputs.visibleRule: command = workspace
inputs.helpMarkDown: The name of the workspace to create, select, or delete.

inputs.name: stateSubCommand
inputs.label: State sub-command
inputs.visibleRule: command = state
inputs.helpMarkDown: The state sub-command to run.

inputs.name: stateAddress
inputs.label: State address / arguments
inputs.visibleRule: command = state
inputs.helpMarkDown: Resource address(es) for the state sub-command. For mv: 'SOURCE DESTINATION'. For rm/show/list: 'ADDRESS'. For push: path to a local state file.

inputs.name: fmtCheck
inputs.label: Check mode (fail if formatting needed)
inputs.visibleRule: command = fmt
inputs.helpMarkDown: If true, runs terraform fmt -check and fails if any files need reformatting. Useful for CI enforcement.

inputs.name: fmtRecursive
inputs.label: Recursive
inputs.visibleRule: command = fmt
inputs.helpMarkDown: If true, runs terraform fmt -recursive to format files in subdirectories.

inputs.name: outputTo
inputs.label: Output to
inputs.visibleRule: command = show
inputs.helpMarkDown: choose output to file or console.

inputs.name: customCommand
inputs.label: Custom command
inputs.visibleRule: command = custom
inputs.helpMarkDown: The custom terraform command to execute.

inputs.name: outputFormat
inputs.label: Output format
inputs.visibleRule: command = show
inputs.helpMarkDown: choose format of ouput for show cmd.

inputs.name: fileName
inputs.label: Output Filename
inputs.visibleRule: outputTo = file
inputs.helpMarkDown: filename of output

inputs.name: environmentServiceNameAzureRM
inputs.label: Azure Provider Service Connection
inputs.visibleRule: provider = azurerm && command != init && command != validate && command != workspace && command != state && command != fmt && command != get
inputs.helpMarkDown: Select an Azure Resource Manager Service Connection for the Azure providers

inputs.name: environmentAzureRmOverrideSubscriptionID
inputs.label: Subscription ID Override
inputs.visibleRule: provider = azurerm && command != init && command != validate && command != workspace && command != state && command != fmt && command != get
inputs.helpMarkDown: Used to override the subscription ID that the provider will target by default. This is set to the ARM_SUBSCRIPTION_ID environment variable. It will use the service connection subscription ID by default.

inputs.name: environmentAzureRmUseIdTokenGeneration
inputs.label: Use ID Token Generation as a fallback for older provider versions
inputs.helpMarkDown: Choose whether to use ID Token generation as a fallback for older provider versions. Warning this setting may result in unexpected timeouts.

inputs.name: environmentServiceNameAWS
inputs.label: Amazon Web Services connection
inputs.visibleRule: provider = aws && command != init && command != validate && command != workspace && command != state && command != fmt && command != get
inputs.helpMarkDown:
Select an Amazon Web Services connection for the deployment.

Note: If your connection is not listed or if you want to use an existing connection, you can setup an Amazon Web Services service connection using the 'Add' or 'Manage' button.

inputs.name: environmentAuthSchemeAWS
inputs.label: Authentication scheme
inputs.visibleRule: provider = aws && command != init && command != validate && command != workspace && command != state && command != fmt && command != get
inputs.helpMarkDown: Select how to authenticate with AWS. 'Service connection' uses static credentials from the service connection. 'Workload Identity Federation' uses OIDC to assume an IAM role without static credentials.

inputs.name: awsRoleArn
inputs.label: IAM Role ARN
inputs.visibleRule: provider = aws && environmentAuthSchemeAWS = WorkloadIdentityFederation && command != init && command != validate && command != workspace && command != state && command != fmt && command != get
inputs.helpMarkDown: ARN of the AWS IAM role to assume via OIDC. E.g. arn:aws:iam::123456789012:role/MyTerraformRole

inputs.name: awsRegion
inputs.label: AWS Region
inputs.visibleRule: provider = aws && environmentAuthSchemeAWS = WorkloadIdentityFederation && command != init && command != validate && command != workspace && command != state && command != fmt && command != get
inputs.helpMarkDown: AWS region for the provider. E.g. us-east-1

inputs.name: awsSessionName
inputs.label: Session name
inputs.visibleRule: provider = aws && environmentAuthSchemeAWS = WorkloadIdentityFederation && command != init && command != validate && command != workspace && command != state && command != fmt && command != get
inputs.helpMarkDown: Name for the assumed role session. Defaults to 'AzureDevOps-Terraform'.

inputs.name: environmentServiceNameGCP
inputs.label: Google Cloud Platform connection
inputs.visibleRule: provider = gcp && command != init && command != validate && command != workspace && command != state && command != fmt && command != get
inputs.helpMarkDown:
Select a Google Cloud Platform connection for the deployment.

Note: If your connection is not listed or if you want to use an existing connection, you can setup a Google Cloud Platform service connection using the 'Add' or 'Manage' button.

inputs.name: environmentAuthSchemeGCP
inputs.label: Authentication scheme
inputs.visibleRule: provider = gcp && command != init && command != validate && command != workspace && command != state && command != fmt && command != get
inputs.helpMarkDown: Select how to authenticate with GCP. 'Service connection' uses a service account key. 'Workload Identity Federation' uses OIDC without static credentials.

inputs.name: gcpProjectNumber
inputs.label: GCP Project Number
inputs.visibleRule: provider = gcp && environmentAuthSchemeGCP = WorkloadIdentityFederation && command != init && command != validate && command != workspace && command != state && command != fmt && command != get
inputs.helpMarkDown: Numeric GCP project number where the Workload Identity Pool lives.

inputs.name: gcpWorkloadIdentityPoolId
inputs.label: Workload Identity Pool ID
inputs.visibleRule: provider = gcp && environmentAuthSchemeGCP = WorkloadIdentityFederation && command != init && command != validate && command != workspace && command != state && command != fmt && command != get
inputs.helpMarkDown: ID of the Workload Identity Pool configured to trust Azure DevOps.

inputs.name: gcpWorkloadIdentityProviderId
inputs.label: Workload Identity Provider ID
inputs.visibleRule: provider = gcp && environmentAuthSchemeGCP = WorkloadIdentityFederation && command != init && command != validate && command != workspace && command != state && command != fmt && command != get
inputs.helpMarkDown: ID of the OIDC provider within the Workload Identity Pool.

inputs.name: gcpServiceAccountEmail
inputs.label: Service Account Email
inputs.visibleRule: provider = gcp && environmentAuthSchemeGCP = WorkloadIdentityFederation && command != init && command != validate && command != workspace && command != state && command != fmt && command != get
inputs.helpMarkDown: Email of the GCP service account to impersonate. E.g. terraform@my-project.iam.gserviceaccount.com

inputs.name: environmentServiceNameOCI
inputs.label: Oracle Cloud Platform connection
inputs.visibleRule: provider = oci && command != init && command != validate && command != workspace && command != state && command != fmt && command != get
inputs.helpMarkDown:
Select a Oracle Cloud Platform connection for the deployment.

Note: If your connection is not listed or if you want to use an existing connection, you can setup a Oracle Cloud Platform service connection using the 'Add' or 'Manage' button.

inputs.name: backendAzureRmUseEntraIdForAuthentication
inputs.label: Use Entra ID for Authentication
inputs.helpMarkDown: Choose whether to use Entra Id authentication to the storage account. If selected, the use_azuread_auth variable will be set to true.

inputs.name: backendAzureRmUseCliFlagsForAuthentication
inputs.label: Use CLI flags for Authentication
inputs.helpMarkDown: Choose whether to use CLI flags for client_id, ado_pipeline_service_connection_id and use_oidc inputs. This is designed to enable the use of a separate service connection for the backend configuration. Defaults to true.

inputs.name: backendAzureRmUseIdTokenGeneration
inputs.label: Use ID Token Generation as a fallback for older backend versions
inputs.helpMarkDown: Choose whether to use ID Token generation as a fallback for older backend versions. Warning this setting may result in unexpected timeouts.

inputs.name: backendServiceArm
inputs.label: Azure Backend Service Connection
inputs.helpMarkDown: Select an Azure Resource Manager Service Connection for the terraform azurerm backend configuration

inputs.name: backendAzureRmOverrideSubscriptionID
inputs.label: Subscription ID Override
inputs.helpMarkDown: Used to override the ID of the subscription which contains the storage account. (Only required for URI lookup)

inputs.name: backendAzureRmResourceGroupName
inputs.label: Resource group
inputs.helpMarkDown: The name of the resource group which contains the storage account. (Only required for URI lookup)

inputs.name: backendAzureRmStorageAccountName
inputs.label: Storage account
inputs.helpMarkDown: The name of the storage account which contains the Azure Blob container.

inputs.name: backendAzureRmContainerName
inputs.label: Container
inputs.helpMarkDown: The name of the Azure Blob container in which to store the Terraform remote state file.

inputs.name: backendAzureRmKey
inputs.label: Key
inputs.helpMarkDown:
The path to the Terraform remote state file inside the container.
For example, if you want to store the state file, named terraform.tfstate, inside a folder, named tf, then give the input 'tf/terraform.tfstate'

inputs.name: backendServiceAWS
inputs.label: Amazon Web Services connection
inputs.helpMarkDown:
Amazon Web Services connection for the terraform backend configuration.

Note: If your connection is not listed or if you want to use an existing connection, you can setup an Amazon Web Services service connection using the 'Add' or 'Manage' button.

inputs.name: backendAWSBucketName
inputs.label: Bucket
inputs.helpMarkDown:
The name of the Amazon Simple Storage Service(S3) bucket for storing the Terraform remote state file.

NOTE: The region of this bucket (e.g. us-east-1) must be specified in the AWS service connection that you must set up. To set up an AWS service connection, click on the 'Help(i)' button of the above input named 'Amazon Web Services connection'

inputs.name: backendAWSKey
inputs.label: Key
inputs.helpMarkDown:
The path to the state file inside the Amazon S3 bucket.
For example, if you want to store the state file, named terraform.tfstate, inside a folder, named tf, then give the input 'tf/terraform.tfstate'.

inputs.name: backendServiceGCP
inputs.label: Google Cloud Platform connection
inputs.helpMarkDown:
Google Cloud Platform connection for the terraform backend configuration.

Note: If your connection is not listed or if you want to use an existing connection, you can setup a Google Cloud Platform service connection using the 'Add' or 'Manage' button.

inputs.name: backendGCPBucketName
inputs.label: Bucket
inputs.helpMarkDown: The name of the GCP storage bucket for storing the Terraform remote state file.

inputs.name: backendGCPPrefix
inputs.label: Prefix of state file
inputs.helpMarkDown: The relative path to the state file inside the GCP bucket. For example, if you give the input as 'terraform', then the state file, named default.tfstate, will be stored inside an object called terraform.

inputs.name: backendHCPToken
inputs.label: HCP Terraform token
inputs.helpMarkDown: API token for HCP Terraform / Terraform Cloud. Generate at app.terraform.io → User Settings → Tokens. This sets the TF_TOKEN_app_terraform_io environment variable.

inputs.name: backendHCPOrganization
inputs.label: Organization name
inputs.helpMarkDown: Optional. HCP Terraform organization name. If not set, Terraform reads the organization from the cloud{} block in your .tf files. Sets TF_CLOUD_ORGANIZATION.

inputs.name: backendHCPWorkspace
inputs.label: Workspace name
inputs.helpMarkDown: Optional. HCP Terraform workspace name. If not set, Terraform reads the workspace from the cloud{} block in your .tf files. Sets TF_WORKSPACE.

inputs.name: backendConfigFile
inputs.label: Backend config file (.tfbackend)
inputs.helpMarkDown: Path to a Terraform backend configuration file (.tfbackend). Passed as -backend-config=<file> to terraform init. Can be used alone or together with the key=value pairs below.

inputs.name: backendConfigArgs
inputs.label: Backend configuration (key=value pairs)
inputs.helpMarkDown:
One backend-config argument per line in key=value format. Each line is passed as a separate -backend-config flag to terraform init. Lines starting with # are ignored as comments.

Examples:
  HTTP backend:   address=https://my-server/state
  PostgreSQL:     conn_str=postgres://user:pass@host/db?sslmode=disable

inputs.name: backendServiceOCI
inputs.label: Oracle Cloud Infrastructure connection
inputs.helpMarkDown:
Oracle Cloud Infrastructure connection for the terraform backend configuration.

Note: If your connection is not listed or if you want to use an existing connection, you can setup a Oracle Cloud Platform service connection using the 'Add' or 'Manage' button.

inputs.name: backendOCIPar
inputs.label: PAR for Terraform remote state file
inputs.helpMarkDown: The OCI object storage PAR configuration for the Terraform remote state file (optional)

inputs.name: backendOCIConfigGenerate
inputs.label: Generate the Terraform remote state file config (Use Yes when not included in TF files)
inputs.helpMarkDown: Generates the Terraform remote state file config, select Yes when not included in TF files, othwerwise No.

outputVariables.name: jsonPlanFilePath
outputVariables.description:
The location of the terraform plan file in JSON format that was created. This file can be used by tasks which are written for tools such as [Open Policy Agent](https://www.openpolicyagent.org/docs/latest/terraform/)

Note: This variable will only be set if 'command' input is set to 'plan'.

outputVariables.name: jsonOutputVariablesPath
outputVariables.description:
The location of the JSON file which contains the output variables set by the user in the terraform config files.

Note: This variable will only be set if 'command' input is set to 'output'.

outputVariables.name: changesPresent
outputVariables.description: A boolean indicating if the terraform plan found any changes to apply.

messages.TerraformToolNotFound: Failed to find terraform tool in paths
